meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement SaaS pricing to overcome the no-portfolio trap. An advanced guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for your first real-world project.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' problem, build a SaaS with a functional, multi-tier pricing engine. By integrating Stripe via Antigravity and Claude Code, you demonstrate real-world revenue logic. This moves you from a 'tutorial follower' to an 'engineer' who understands the complexities of billing, webhooks, and user subscription states.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of junior developer careers. You’ve built the to-do lists and the weather apps, but recruiters are looking for the 'aha' moment—the ability to handle real-world business logic. Without a functional, revenue-generating architecture, your work remains academic, invisible to the eyes that matter.

We are going to change that. By architecting a robust SaaS pricing model, you aren't just writing code; you're building a business case. Using the surgical precision of Claude Code and the abstraction power of Antigravity, we will transform your project from a static repository into a professional-grade SaaS engine that proves your technical maturity.

steps.title: Define Tiered Subscription Schema
steps.description: Establish the data structure for Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers. You need to handle state transitions between these tiers securely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Prisma schema for a SaaS user model that includes subscription_status, stripe_customer_id, and tier_level enums.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize Prisma schema with tiered subscription fields'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Checkout Integration
steps.description: Create the server-side logic to handle session creation for Stripe. This is the heart of your SaaS monetization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Next.js API route to create a Stripe Checkout session based on a selected price_id.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create stripe-checkout.ts API route with secure session handling'

steps.title: Build Robust Webhook Listeners
steps.description: Handle asynchronous events from Stripe to update user database records automatically when payments succeed or fail.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Develop a Stripe webhook handler to process invoice.payment_succeeded and customer.subscription.deleted events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement stripe-webhook.ts to sync subscription status with DB'

steps.title: Develop Middleware Access Control
steps.description: Restrict premium features based on the user's subscription tier using edge middleware for performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware function that checks the user's tier_level and redirects unauthorized users from pro-only routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add route-level protection middleware for premium features'

steps.title: Audit and Security Hardening
steps.description: Ensure your pricing logic is tamper-proof and that database updates are atomic and secure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review current implementation for potential race conditions in subscription updates and add input validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Audit subscription logic for security vulnerabilities and race conditions'

pro_tips: Always store Stripe price IDs as environment variables, never hardcode them in your client-side components.
Use a 'grace period' logic in your database to prevent immediate lockout if a payment fails due to transient network issues.
Implement a 'Trial Ended' state explicitly to trigger automated email re-engagement flows.
Never trust the client-side state for feature access; always perform a server-side check against the source of truth (your DB).
Use Stripe CLI to simulate webhooks locally; it is the only way to effectively debug production-grade subscription flows.
content.next_steps: Once the pricing engine is live, deploy your project to Vercel and add a 'Live Demo' link to your GitHub README. Recruiters want to see the product, not just the code.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to build a project that commands respect and demonstrates real-world engineering capability. Stop waiting for permission to be a professional. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
